name: Panama Canal Zone (1903-1979)

colors.symbolism: the Atlantic and Pacific oceans linked by the canal

description: A navy-blue field bearing the circular seal of the Panama Canal Zone in the centre — a gold disc with a Spanish galleon between two coasts on a blue and white sea, surrounded by a gold-bordered ring inscribed with the territory's name and the motto 'The Land Divided, The World United'.
history: The Panama Canal Zone was a 10-mile-wide US-administered strip of land flanking the Panama Canal, leased in perpetuity from Panama by the 1903 Hay-Bunau-Varilla Treaty after Panama's independence from Colombia. The Zone used United States flags and ensigns in administration; the territorial seal flag illustrated here served as the distinctive emblem of the civilian Canal Zone government. Following the 1977 Torrijos-Carter Treaties, the Canal Zone was abolished on 1 October 1979 and its territory returned to Panamanian sovereignty; the canal itself was operated under joint Panama-US arrangements until full transfer to Panama on 31 December 1999.

features.symbolism: the deep waters of the two oceans the canal joins

features.role: Seal of the Panama Canal Zone
features.symbolism: the official seal of the Canal Zone — a Spanish galleon under sail between two land masses below a banner reading 'The Land Divided, The World United', representing the canal's joining of the two oceans and the achievement of US engineering
